info.title: DESCRIPTION
info.body: BOYSEN®® AcquaPro B‑6715 is a 100% acrylic water-based paint formulated as a topcoat for properly primed wood and metal surfaces. It is specifically designed for areas where paint odor should be minimized when painting substrates made of wood and metal. This high-performance environment-friendly coating delivers a long-lasting, elegant sheen while offering excellent adhesion, fast drying time, and superior durability.

info.title: FEATURES
info.body:
Water-Based Formula - Low-odor and easy to
clean up with water.
Durable Finish - Provides a hard-wearing,
chip-resistant coating.
Quick Drying - Faster drying time allows for
quicker project turnaround.
Excellent Adhesion - Bonds strongly to
primed wood, primed metal, and previously
painted surfaces.
Weather and Rust Resistant - Suitable for
both indoor and outdoor applications.
Eco-Friendly - Safer for the environment and
improves indoor air quality.

info.title: PRINCIPAL USES
info.body: Ideal for doors, furniture, railings, cabinets, trims, and other surfaces, BOYSEN®® AcquaPro B‑6715 provides a finish with rich color vibrancy and long-term protection.

info.title: PAINTING SCHEDULE
info.subtitle: Wooden Surfaces
info.body: Primer: BOYSEN®® WoodPrime™ B‑180 Putty: BOYSEN®® Masonry Putty B‑7311 2nd & 3rd coat: BOYSEN®® AcquaPro B‑6715

info.title: PAINTING SCHEDULE
info.subtitle: Metal Surfaces
info.body: Primer: BOYSEN®® Red Oxide Metal Primer B‑310 or BOYSEN®® Rust-Oï¬€ B‑330 2nd & 3rd coat: BOYSEN®® AcquaPro B‑6715

info.title: SURFACE PREPARATION
info.subtitle: Wooden Surfaces
info.body: Surface to be painted should be clean and dry, free from dust, dirt, and other foreign matter. Hammer all nails deep enough to apply putty. Sand rough wood until smooth and then dust oï¬€ before painting. For repainting, scrape oï¬€ all loose, scaling, flaking, and peeling paint as necessary. Make sure the surface is clean, dry, and good weather persists prior to painting. Prime with BOYSEN®® WoodPrime™ before recoating.

info.title: SURFACE PREPARATION
info.subtitle: Metal Surfaces
info.body: Wipe the surface with a rag soaked in paint thinner to remove grease, oil, wax, and other foreign matter. For new galvanized surfaces, etch the surface by applying 10% BOYSEN®® Metal Etching Solution B‑71 (Dilute 1 part of B‑71 to 9 parts water). Rinse with water and dry immediately. Unpainted galvanized surfaces that have been exposed for 6 months or more need not be etched. For recoating pre-painted galvanized surfaces in good condition, sand lightly then wipe the surface with a clean rag before applying paint. Remove all loose paint film to sound surface. Spot prime bare substrate. For corroded galvanized surfaces, remove rust by wire brushing and use BOYSEN®® Metal Etching Solution B‑71. Let it stay for 10 to 15 minutes. Be sure to remove all remaining residue. Immediately prime after drying.
